body.u: It will soon be my seventy fifth birthday, three quarters of a century I’ve lived. I’ll be gone soon, and all that will be left of me will be a gravestone and memories. Memories. I can remember when I left school. Seventeen I was, I thought I was getting on then, now look at me. Still we had a good life I must admit, I’ve enjoyed myself.
When I left school I had the idea that I would walk straight into a good job with good money and then, in a couple of years, get married looking at the world through rose coloured glasses.
For the first six months I couldn’t get a job anywhere then i got one as a typist in a typing pool in an office somewhere in London the pay wasn’t very good, but it was enough.

After a while I was fed up with that and decided to move on. The next job I had I had moved up in the world. I was a secretary in large office block just outside Kensington it was a nice job, nice pay, nice area but I was bored with it all, I wanted excitement I stuck that job for a year and a half and then after an argument with the boss I was made redundant, oh well cantcan't win them all.

During my period of redundancy I met Robert, he liked me, I liked him so we got married, I’m still with him now. Just after I was married I got another job as a personal secretary to the head man of some big firm or other. I didn’t look back. It was wonderful job full of excitement and travelling about, just what I had wanted and it had good pay. Then I got pregnant and had two years off to look after the baby. When I asked my boss for my job back, he gave it to me gladly he said that he hadn’t managed to get anyone as good as me since I had left. I stayed with that firm for the rest of my working days, and when I retired I recievedreceived a good pension from them. ItsIt's wonderful what you can achieve if you work for it, but you have to work very hard.
Oh well I’ve had my trip down memory lane now. I suppose I’ve bored you all but its nice to look back, and think of all the wonderful times I had.
